description: Full ZORBA industry and sub-industry taxonomy for common enterprise segmentation, benchmarking, and AI-guided strategy-to-execution workflows.

industries.name: Agriculture & Food Production
industries.definition: Organisations involved in growing, raising, processing, and distributing food and agricultural products.

subIndustries.name: Crop Farming & Agronomy
subIndustries.definition: Businesses cultivating field crops, horticulture, and specialty crops, including agronomic advisory and input services.

subIndustries.name: Livestock & Animal Husbandry
subIndustries.definition: Businesses raising animals for meat, dairy, wool, and other products, including breeding and veterinary services.

subIndustries.name: Food Processing & Manufacturing
subIndustries.definition: Businesses that transform raw agricultural inputs into packaged food and beverage products for distribution and retail.

subIndustries.name: AgriTech & Precision Agriculture
subIndustries.definition: Technology businesses providing digital tools, sensors, automation, and data platforms to modernise agricultural operations.

subIndustries.name: Agri-Commodities & Trading
subIndustries.definition: Businesses involved in the trading, brokerage, and logistics of agricultural commodities across global markets.

subIndustries.name: Aquaculture & Fisheries
subIndustries.definition: Businesses farming or harvesting aquatic organisms including fish, shellfish, and seaweed for food and other products.

industries.name: Education & Non-Profit
industries.definition: Organisations focused on learning, research, social impact, and mission-led service delivery.

subIndustries.name: Higher Education
subIndustries.definition: Universities, colleges, and institutions providing tertiary education and research.

subIndustries.name: K-12 Education
subIndustries.definition: Schools and education bodies serving primary and secondary learners.

subIndustries.name: EdTech Platforms
subIndustries.definition: Technology businesses providing digital learning, academic administration, or educational support solutions.

subIndustries.name: Non-Profit & NGOs
subIndustries.definition: Mission-led organisations delivering social, humanitarian, advocacy, or development outcomes.

subIndustries.name: Research Institutions
subIndustries.definition: Institutions dedicated to academic, scientific, or policy research and knowledge creation.

industries.name: Energy & Utilities
industries.definition: Organisations involved in energy production, distribution, trading, and utility services.

subIndustries.name: Oil & Gas
subIndustries.definition: Businesses exploring, extracting, refining, transporting, or selling hydrocarbons.

subIndustries.name: Renewable Energy
subIndustries.definition: Businesses generating or managing power from renewable sources such as solar, wind, and hydro.

subIndustries.name: Utilities
subIndustries.definition: Regulated providers of essential services such as electricity, water, and gas.

subIndustries.name: Energy Trading & Markets
subIndustries.definition: Businesses engaged in trading power, gas, and energy-related contracts in wholesale markets.

industries.name: Financial Services
industries.definition: Institutions that manage money, credit, investment, insurance, and financial transactions.

subIndustries.name: Retail Banking
subIndustries.definition: Banks serving consumers and small businesses with deposits, payments, and lending products.

subIndustries.name: Corporate & Investment Banking
subIndustries.definition: Financial institutions serving corporates and investors with advisory, capital markets, and structured finance services.

subIndustries.name: Wealth & Asset Management
subIndustries.definition: Firms managing client investments, portfolios, and advisory relationships.

subIndustries.name: Insurance
subIndustries.definition: Insurers and intermediaries that price, underwrite, and service risk protection products.

subIndustries.name: Payments & Fintech
subIndustries.definition: Digital financial businesses providing payment, wallet, transaction, or embedded finance services.

subIndustries.name: Capital Markets Infrastructure
subIndustries.definition: Businesses providing exchanges, clearing, settlement, custody, and market infrastructure services.

subIndustries.name: Private Equity & Venture Capital
subIndustries.definition: Investment firms providing capital, strategic guidance, and operational support to private companies through equity ownership.

industries.name: Healthcare & Life Sciences
industries.definition: Organisations focused on health delivery, research, therapeutics, diagnostics, and medical innovation.

subIndustries.name: Hospitals & Care Providers
subIndustries.definition: Care delivery organisations providing treatment, diagnostics, and patient services.

subIndustries.name: Pharmaceuticals
subIndustries.definition: Companies researching, manufacturing, and commercialising therapeutic drugs.

subIndustries.name: Biotechnology
subIndustries.definition: Science-led organisations using biological systems to develop treatments, diagnostics, or enabling technologies.

subIndustries.name: Medical Devices
subIndustries.definition: Manufacturers and innovators of medical equipment, diagnostics, and treatment devices.

subIndustries.name: Digital Health & Telemedicine
subIndustries.definition: Technology-enabled healthcare services and platforms supporting remote care, monitoring, and digital workflows.

subIndustries.name: Health Insurance / Payers
subIndustries.definition: Organisations financing healthcare access through insurance products, networks, and claims administration.

subIndustries.name: Clinical Research Organisations
subIndustries.definition: Organisations providing outsourced clinical trial management, regulatory support, and research services to pharmaceutical and biotech companies.

industries.name: Hospitality, Travel & Leisure
industries.definition: Businesses providing experiences, accommodation, travel, food service, and leisure activities.

subIndustries.name: Hotels & Accommodation
subIndustries.definition: Businesses providing lodging, guest services, and accommodation experiences.

subIndustries.name: Travel & Tourism Operators
subIndustries.definition: Businesses packaging, selling, or operating travel experiences, tours, and related services.

subIndustries.name: Restaurants & Food Services
subIndustries.definition: Businesses preparing and serving food through dine-in, takeaway, catering, or quick-service models.

subIndustries.name: Entertainment & Leisure
subIndustries.definition: Businesses providing events, attractions, gaming, recreation, or leisure experiences.

industries.name: Manufacturing & Industrial
industries.definition: Businesses that produce physical goods, components, and industrial systems at scale.

subIndustries.name: Discrete Manufacturing
subIndustries.definition: Manufacturing environments producing countable units such as vehicles, machinery, and equipment.

subIndustries.name: Process Manufacturing
subIndustries.definition: Manufacturing environments producing bulk or formula-based outputs such as chemicals, food, and materials.

subIndustries.name: Industrial Equipment & Machinery
subIndustries.definition: Manufacturers of machinery, capital equipment, and industrial systems used by other businesses.

subIndustries.name: Electronics & Semiconductors
subIndustries.definition: Businesses designing or manufacturing electronic components, chips, and digital hardware systems.

subIndustries.name: Aerospace & Defense Manufacturing
subIndustries.definition: Manufacturers of aircraft, defense systems, and associated industrial platforms.

subIndustries.name: Supply Chain & Logistics Operations
subIndustries.definition: Operational businesses focused on warehousing, movement, and fulfilment of physical goods at scale.

subIndustries.name: Automotive
subIndustries.definition: Businesses designing, manufacturing, and selling motor vehicles, components, and automotive systems.

industries.name: Mining & Natural Resources
industries.definition: Organisations that extract, process, and manage natural resources including minerals, metals, timber, and water.

subIndustries.name: Metals & Mineral Mining
subIndustries.definition: Businesses extracting and processing metallic ores and industrial minerals from the earth.

subIndustries.name: Forestry & Timber
subIndustries.definition: Businesses managing forests and producing timber, pulp, and wood-based products.

subIndustries.name: Water Resources & Management
subIndustries.definition: Organisations managing water extraction, treatment, distribution, and conservation for industrial, agricultural, and municipal use.

subIndustries.name: Mining Services & Equipment
subIndustries.definition: Businesses providing specialised services, equipment, and technology to mining operations.

subIndustries.name: Quarrying & Aggregates
subIndustries.definition: Businesses extracting stone, sand, gravel, and other aggregate materials for construction and industrial use.

industries.name: Professional Services
industries.definition: Knowledge-intensive firms that deliver expertise, advice, and specialised services to clients on a project or retainer basis.

subIndustries.name: Management Consulting
subIndustries.definition: Firms advising organisations on strategy, operations, organisational design, and transformation.

subIndustries.name: Technology Consulting & Systems Integration
subIndustries.definition: Firms advising on technology strategy and delivering implementation, integration, and managed service engagements.

subIndustries.name: Legal Services
subIndustries.definition: Law firms and legal service providers delivering advisory, transactional, dispute resolution, and compliance services.

subIndustries.name: Accounting & Audit
subIndustries.definition: Firms providing audit, assurance, tax advisory, bookkeeping, and financial consulting services.

subIndustries.name: Architecture & Engineering Services
subIndustries.definition: Design and engineering consultancies providing architectural, structural, civil, environmental, and planning services.

subIndustries.name: Recruitment & Staffing
subIndustries.definition: Firms providing talent acquisition, executive search, contingent staffing, and workforce solutions.

subIndustries.name: Marketing & Creative Agencies
subIndustries.definition: Agencies delivering brand strategy, creative production, digital marketing, public relations, and communications services.

subIndustries.name: BPO & Shared Services
subIndustries.definition: Firms providing outsourced business processes, contact centres, back-office operations, and global business services.

industries.name: Public Sector & Government
industries.definition: Government bodies and public institutions delivering public services, regulation, and civic infrastructure.

subIndustries.name: Central Government
subIndustries.definition: National-level government departments and agencies delivering policy, regulation, and public administration.

subIndustries.name: Local Government & Municipalities
subIndustries.definition: Regional and local authorities delivering community services, permitting, and infrastructure operations.

subIndustries.name: Defense & Security
subIndustries.definition: Public organisations responsible for national defense, intelligence, and security operations.

subIndustries.name: Public Health & Education
subIndustries.definition: Publicly funded institutions delivering health, education, and community development services.

subIndustries.name: Smart Cities & Infrastructure
subIndustries.definition: Public programmes and entities managing digital infrastructure, transport, utilities, and connected civic systems.

industries.name: Real Estate & Construction
industries.definition: Businesses that develop, manage, finance, transact, or build physical property and infrastructure.

subIndustries.name: Commercial Real Estate
subIndustries.definition: Businesses owning, leasing, or managing office, retail, industrial, or mixed-use commercial property.

subIndustries.name: Residential Real Estate
subIndustries.definition: Businesses managing, developing, or transacting residential property and housing portfolios.

subIndustries.name: Property Management
subIndustries.definition: Operators managing buildings, tenant services, maintenance, and facilities on behalf of owners or residents.

subIndustries.name: Construction & Engineering
subIndustries.definition: Businesses delivering construction, infrastructure, and engineering projects for public and private clients.

subIndustries.name: PropTech
subIndustries.definition: Technology businesses digitising real estate transactions, property operations, or building experiences.

industries.name: Retail & Consumer Goods
industries.definition: Businesses that design, market, distribute, and sell goods directly or indirectly to consumers.

subIndustries.name: E-commerce & Marketplaces
subIndustries.definition: Digital commerce businesses selling products online directly or through multi-sided platforms.

subIndustries.name: Brick-and-Mortar Retail
subIndustries.definition: Retailers serving customers through physical stores and in-person shopping experiences.

subIndustries.name: Consumer Packaged Goods
subIndustries.definition: Brands and manufacturers producing packaged goods for sale through retail and distribution channels.

subIndustries.name: Luxury & Fashion
subIndustries.definition: Brands and retailers selling premium fashion, accessories, and lifestyle goods.

subIndustries.name: Direct-to-Consumer Brands
subIndustries.definition: Consumer brands selling directly to end buyers through owned digital and physical channels.

industries.name: Technology & Software
industries.definition: Organisations that create, deliver, or enable digital products, platforms, and infrastructure.

subIndustries.name: Enterprise Software
subIndustries.definition: Software companies serving business customers with applications, workflows, and operational platforms.

subIndustries.name: Vertical SaaS
subIndustries.definition: Software businesses focused on a specific industry or niche operating model.

subIndustries.name: Cloud Infrastructure
subIndustries.definition: Providers of infrastructure, platforms, hosting, and cloud-native runtime services.

subIndustries.name: Cybersecurity
subIndustries.definition: Organisations delivering security software, services, or managed protection capabilities.

subIndustries.name: Data & Analytics Platforms
subIndustries.definition: Platforms that help organisations collect, process, analyse, and operationalise data.

subIndustries.name: AI & Machine Learning Platforms
subIndustries.definition: Platforms and products enabling AI model development, deployment, orchestration, and business automation.

subIndustries.name: Developer Tools & DevOps
subIndustries.definition: Tools and platforms used by software teams to build, test, deploy, and operate software.

subIndustries.name: Consumer Apps & Platforms
subIndustries.definition: Digital products and platforms serving consumers at scale through apps, subscriptions, or marketplaces.

subIndustries.name: IT Infrastructure & Hardware
subIndustries.definition: Businesses designing, manufacturing, and selling servers, networking equipment, storage hardware, and related infrastructure components.

industries.name: Telecommunications & Media
industries.definition: Businesses that connect people and distribute information, communications, and content.

subIndustries.name: Telecom Operators
subIndustries.definition: Providers of mobile, broadband, and communications services to consumers and enterprises.

subIndustries.name: Network Infrastructure Providers
subIndustries.definition: Businesses that build, lease, or manage telecom network infrastructure and related systems.

subIndustries.name: Media Production & Studios
subIndustries.definition: Businesses creating film, television, audio, and other media content for distribution.

subIndustries.name: Streaming & Digital Media Platforms
subIndustries.definition: Digital platforms distributing content to audiences through subscription, advertising, or hybrid models.

subIndustries.name: Advertising & Publishing
subIndustries.definition: Businesses monetising audiences through advertising, sponsorship, publishing, and brand services.

subIndustries.name: Gaming & Interactive Entertainment
subIndustries.definition: Businesses creating, publishing, and distributing video games, interactive media, and immersive entertainment experiences.

industries.name: Transportation & Logistics
industries.definition: Organisations that move goods and people across local, national, and global networks.

subIndustries.name: Airlines & Aviation
subIndustries.definition: Businesses transporting passengers or cargo by air and supporting aviation operations.

subIndustries.name: Shipping & Maritime
subIndustries.definition: Businesses moving goods or people by sea, including shipping lines, ports, and maritime operators.

subIndustries.name: Freight & Logistics Providers
subIndustries.definition: Businesses arranging or operating freight, warehousing, and multi-modal logistics services.

subIndustries.name: Rail & Public Transport
subIndustries.definition: Passenger or freight transport services delivered through rail and public transit systems.

subIndustries.name: Last-Mile Delivery
subIndustries.definition: Businesses specialising in local delivery of parcels, food, or goods to end recipients.
